name: Weekly Macro Desk
title: Weekly Macro Trading Report
preview: DAX / NQ / ES / XAUUSD / Silver / EURUSD / GBPUSD / GBPAUD...
fullMessage:
Macro regime:
Late-cycle disinflation, slowing growth, equity markets still dependent on AI and mega-cap liquidity, with high sensitivity to rate repricing.

Primary drivers:
1. US labor market repricing: ADP and NFP
2. ISM Manufacturing and Services
3. Eurozone Flash CPI
4. US bond yield volatility
5. USD repricing through Fed expectations

Institutional positioning:
Equities remain crowded long. Gold positioning is elevated but supported by rate-cut expectations. USD positioning is lighter than earlier months, which creates risk of sharp short-covering if US data beats.

name: Monday Setup
title: ISM Manufacturing: first major risk event
preview: Highest volatility expected around the NY data window...
fullMessage:
Key events:
China PMIs during Asia session. ISM Manufacturing and ISM Manufacturing Prices at 16:00.

Most important event:
ISM Manufacturing PMI. The market will care more about prices paid, new orders and employment than the headline number.

Bullish risk-on scenario:
If ISM is weak enough to support rate cuts but not weak enough to signal recession, and prices paid cools, NASDAQ can rally, DAX can follow, Gold can stay bid, USD can soften and yields can move lower.

Bearish risk-off scenario:
If prices remain hot and the headline or employment subindex beats, yields can spike, USD can rally, Gold can dump initially and equities can come under pressure.

name: Tuesday Setup
title: Eurozone CPI and JOLTS drive EUR / DAX divergence
preview: London CPI first, then US labor-market repricing in NY...
fullMessage:
Key events:
Eurozone Flash CPI at 11:00. JOLTS Job Openings at 16:00.

Best intraday opportunity:
EURUSD and DAX divergence trade.

Bullish EUR scenario:
If Core CPI beats forecast, EUR can rally, DAX can be pressured initially and ECB cuts may be repriced slower.

Bearish EUR scenario:
If CPI is soft, EUR can be sold while DAX may rally on easing hopes. Gold may benefit indirectly if softer yields support metals.

Trap:
Euro CPI often creates a first spike liquidity sweep before the bond market confirms direction.

name: Wednesday Setup
title: ISM Services: most explosive midweek catalyst
preview: Services inflation is still the key Fed-sensitive pressure point...
fullMessage:
Key events:
Australia GDP at 03:30, ADP Employment at 14:15, ISM Services at 16:00 and Beige Book at 20:00.

Most important event:
ISM Services PMI. Expected volatility is extreme because services inflation directly affects rate expectations, yields, USD liquidity and tech valuations.

Bullish risk-on scenario:
If services weaken moderately and prices/employment soften, yields can drop sharply, NASDAQ can squeeze higher and Gold can attract strong bids.

Risk-off scenario:
If ISM Services is hot, a large yield spike is possible. USD can squeeze higher, Gold can become vulnerable and equities can be pressured.

Liquidity trap:
Classic pattern is ADP fake move, reversal, then ISM real move.

name: Thursday Setup
title: Claims and labor costs before NFP
preview: A lower-liquidity session where stop hunts and position squaring matter...
fullMessage:
Key events:
US Initial Claims and Unit Labor Costs at 14:30.

Why it matters:
This is a labor-market plus wage-inflation combo. It can shift Fed expectations before NFP.

Bullish equity scenario:
If claims rise and labor costs cool, yields can move lower, equities can stay supported and Gold can remain bid.

Bearish equity scenario:
If labor costs are hot and claims remain low, the market can reprice higher-for-longer Fed risk. USD and yields can rise.

Risk note:
Thursday before NFP often has lower liquidity, stop hunts and position squaring. Avoid oversized positions.

name: Friday NFP Desk
title: NFP and wages: main event of the week
preview: The market should prioritize wages, unemployment, then payrolls headline...
fullMessage:
Key events:
US Non-Farm Payrolls, Unemployment Rate and Average Hourly Earnings at 14:30.

Priority order:
1. Average Hourly Earnings
2. Unemployment Rate
3. Payrolls headline

Bullish risk-on scenario:
If payrolls are softer, unemployment rises and wages cool, yields can collapse, Gold can explode higher, NASDAQ can rally strongly and USD can sell off aggressively.

USD bullish risk-off scenario:
If payrolls are strong and wages are hot, Fed cuts can be repriced out, yields can surge, USD can squeeze, Gold can drop sharply and equities can become vulnerable.

NFP warning:
Initial moves often stop-hunt and reverse after 5-20 minutes, especially when payrolls and wages contradict each other.

name: Equity Index View
title: DAX, NASDAQ and S&P500 weekly bias
preview: Equities are constructive but fragile if yields spike...
fullMessage:
DAX:
Bias is neutral-to-bullish unless yields spike. Key drivers are EUR weakness and global risk sentiment. Best sessions are London open and NY overlap. Main risk is a stronger EUR after CPI.

NASDAQ:
Bias is bullish but fragile. This is the most rate-sensitive asset this week. ISM and NFP can quickly flip the tone if data is hot.

S&P500:
Bias is mildly bullish and more defensive than NASDAQ. It should still respond strongly to labor data and yields.

name: Metals View
title: Gold and Silver remain bullish but volatile
preview: Metals are supported by rate-cut expectations but vulnerable to USD squeezes...
fullMessage:
Gold:
Medium-term bias is bullish, supported by rate-cut expectations and lower-yield scenarios. The main risk is temporary USD and yield squeezes after strong US data.

Best Gold setup:
Post-data continuation after weak labor or softer inflation details, especially when US10Y confirms lower.

Silver:
High-beta Gold trade with more volatility. It is also sensitive to China PMIs and industrial sentiment. Silver can outperform when both precious metals and growth sentiment align.

name: FX View
title: EURUSD neutral, GBP firmer, GBPAUD bullish
preview: FX direction depends on CPI, labor data and relative central-bank repricing...
fullMessage:
EURUSD:
Bias is data-dependent and neutral. Main drivers are Eurozone CPI and US labor data. Watch stop hunts around London highs and lows after CPI.

GBPUSD:
Bias is mildly bullish versus EUR because BOE pricing remains relatively hawkish due to sticky UK inflation dynamics.

GBPAUD:
Bias is bullish GBP. AUD is vulnerable if China or Australia data disappoints. Best sessions are Asia and London.

name: Weekly Playbook
title: Best days, traps and safer execution rules
preview: Wednesday and Friday offer the strongest momentum conditions...
fullMessage:
Best trading days:
1. Wednesday
2. Friday
3. Tuesday

Worst days to overtrade:
Thursday and Monday before ISM.

Best opportunity:
NASDAQ around ISM and NFP because it has the highest probability momentum profile this week.

Safer setups:
Trade after confirmation, wait for yield confirmation and prefer continuation setups after the first reaction settles.

Aggressive setups:
Fade first NFP spike, pre-position Gold before weak labor expectations or trade EURUSD CPI breakouts. These require tighter risk control.
